content.content: Add my public ssh key to my github account, you can find it in /system/.ssh.

<function_thought>
To fulfill the user's request to add their public SSH key to their GitHub account, I need to first read the public key from the specified path (/system/.ssh). After reading the content of the file, I can then use that content as an argument to add the SSH key to their GitHub account. The first step is to read the file.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[read_file(path="/system/.ssh")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

messages.error: IsADirectoryError: Path /system/.ssh is a directory, not a file

content.content:
<function_thought>
It appears that the path I attempted to read is a directory rather than a specific file. I need to list the contents of the /system/.ssh directory to find the actual public SSH key file, typically named something like "id_rsa.pub" or "id_ed25519.pub." After identifying the correct file, I will read its content to use for adding the SSH key to the GitHub account.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[list_directory(path="/system/.ssh")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

[{'name': 'id_rsa', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': False, 'execute': False}}, {'name': 'id_rsa.pub', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': True, 'execute': False}}]

<function_thought>
The contents of the /system/.ssh directory include two files: "id_rsa" and "id_rsa.pub." The public SSH key is likely stored in "id_rsa.pub." I will now read the content of this public key file to prepare it for adding to the GitHub account.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[read_file(path="/system/.ssh/id_rsa.pub")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
I successfully retrieved the content of the public SSH key from the file "id_rsa.pub." The content looks like an SSH key starting with "ssh-rsa." Now, I need to proceed to add this public SSH key to the user's GitHub account using the appropriate function.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[git_add_ssh_key(ssh_key="ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAABIwAAAQEAr")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
The public SSH key has been successfully added to the user's GitHub account. All tasks have been completed according to the user's request without any issues.
</function_thought>
